window.body:
*** Active remediations are in process to address this threat, review any remaining remediations (such as reboots) before marking this incident as resolved in the Huntress Platform. ***

*** The Huntress Agent has been tasked to isolate this host from the rest of the network in order to prevent the incident from spreading to other hosts. ***

If you have an urgent request for support, please go to the link below to place a request a callback from SOC Support.
https://technijian.huntress.io/org/449055/infection_reports/1929313/soc_callback_requests/new

Host: RMG-LPT-TOM - https://technijian.huntress.io/org/449055/agents/11774957
Organization: Roddel Marketing Group, Inc.
Tags: None
Security Products: CrowdStrike (CSFalconService), Windows Defender

Incident Report: https://technijian.huntress.io/org/449055/infection_reports/1929313
Severity: Critical

Investigative Summary:
----------------------
At 2026-02-03 21:31:35 UTC, Huntress detected malicious activity on host "RMG-LPT-TOM" involving user "Technijian". The user executed a malicious file named "eStmt_2026-1-2658714100.exe" from the Downloads folder, which was spawned by Microsoft Edge ("msedge.exe"). This file is a renamed SimpleHelp remote access tool, disguised as a tax-themed document, consistent with a phishing campaign targeting users with fake Social Security Administration statements.

The malicious executable was launched directly from the user's Downloads folder at 2026-02-03 21:31:35 UTC. The file, signed by "SimpleHelp Ltd" but with an invalid signature, was executed by the user after being downloaded through the Microsoft Edge browser. The filename "eStmt_2026-1-2658714100.exe" mimics a legitimate tax statement, a common social engineering tactic. The process terminated shortly after execution at 2026-02-03 21:31:35 UTC with an abnormal exit code, indicating the initial execution may have failed or was interrupted. However, the presence of this file and the execution attempt indicate the user fell victim to a phishing attack designed to install unauthorized remote access software.

The primary risk is unauthorized remote access to the affected host. Even though the process terminated, the malicious file remains on disk and may have established persistence or could be re-executed. Huntress recommends using Assisted Remediation to remove the malicious file from the Downloads folder. Additionally, review the user's browser history to identify the phishing domain and check for any persistence mechanisms such as registry run keys or scheduled tasks. Provide security awareness training to the user regarding phishing tactics, particularly those involving fake tax documents. Failure to remediate may result in unauthorized access, data theft, or deployment of additional malware.

User Account
User Account: RMG-LPT-TOM\Technijian (SID: S-1-5-21-3431483238-1952811798-1897033814-1001)

File System
File Path: C:\Users\Technijian\Downloads\eStmt_2026-1-2658714100.exe
SHA256: 3e22929618216e03230e991854140bef0e0298c76e21f711d2a21685183f422a
MD5: 4aac5507aee93b3b5a6b06fd2882a5d7
SHA1: 2dd50d8246b5727be73c39fd577c42dfdb6af61e

Host
Hostname: RMG-LPT-TOM
Operating System: Windows 11 Home

Threat Descriptions:
--------------------
Malicious Remote Management Tool: A legitimate remote management tool has been misused by a threat actor to gain unauthorized access and control of the reported endpoint. Threat actors do this to hide their presence, maintain persistence and further exploit the network.

Rogue Simplehelp: Huntress has been tracking a number of malicious threat actors convincing users via email into running malicious Simplehelp installers that give the threat actor remote access to the host.

Remediations:
-------------
Assisted Remediations provided by the Huntress SOC to remediate the incident. These can be executed automatically in the Huntress Platform:
- Delete File - path: C:\Users\Technijian\Downloads\eStmt_2026-1-2658714100.exe + sha256: 3e22929618216e03230e991854140bef0e0298c76e21f711d2a21685183f422a
- Reboot the Host - remediation: A reboot is required to complete the remediation plan

Manual Remediations provided by the Huntress SOC are highly recommended remediation actions to be conducted by your team before resolving the incident in the Huntress Platform:
- Enroll the user in a security awareness training session to reinforce safe practices in the corporate environment.
- Audit affected directories/files for additional suspicious files and remove those found. 

All remediations provided can be found in the Huntress Platform: Incident Report: https://technijian.huntress.io/org/449055/infection_reports/1929313#remediations-tab

Lead Signal Information:
------------------------
Signal Name: Web Browser Process Spawning Tax Themed Rmm
Detected At: 2026-02-03 15:41:14 -0600
Start Time: 2026-02-03 21:31:35 UTC
Command: "C:\Users\Technijian\Downloads\eStmt_2026-1-2658714100.exe"
Executable: C:\Users\Technijian\Downloads\eStmt_2026-1-2658714100.exe
Process ID: f84c723e-0145-11f1-b5a0-201e88d0a290
Parent Process: C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft\Edge\Application\msedge.exe
User: Technijian

All investigated signals can be found in the Huntress Platform: https://technijian.huntress.io/org/449055/infection_reports/1929313#signals-investigated-tab

-------------------------
Thanks again for trusting Huntress and please don't hesitate to reach out to incidents@huntress.io if you have any questions.

items.action: Enroll the user in a security awareness training session to reinforce safe practices in the corporate environment.
A reboot is required to complete the remediation plan
Audit affected directories/files for additional suspicious files and remove those found.

window.subject: CRITICAL - ISOLATED - Incident on RMG-LPT-TOM (Roddel Marketing Group, Inc.)
